name: custom-select
title: Custom Select
description: A styled listbox-select built on Base UI's Select for cases the native Select cannot cover: option groups, disabled options, and rich rows (icon + text). The trigger is a role=combobox button with aria-haspopup=listbox over a listbox popup; APG listbox keyboard (arrows, type-ahead, Home/End, Enter/Space, Escape), a selected-option check plus aria-selected, a forced-colors-safe chevron, Field wiring, and 44px targets. Not filterable — use ComboBox for type-ahead.
useWhen: A single-select where options need grouping under labelled sections (agencies by department, wards by district)
Options need richer content than plain text (an icon, a status marker) that a native <option> cannot render
You need a consistent, themeable dropdown surface across browsers for a short-to-medium option set
avoidWhen: An ordinary short list of plain-text options (use the native Commons Select — it is lighter and uses the platform's own popup)
A long list users need to filter by typing (use the combo-box for type-ahead)
Free-text entry or values not constrained to a list (use an Input)
Fewer than ~5 options where every choice should be visible (use a radio-group)

keyboard: Tab moves focus to the trigger; Shift+Tab moves away
Enter, Space, or Down/Up Arrow opens the listbox
Down Arrow / Up Arrow move the highlight to the next / previous option
Home / End jump to the first / last option
Typing printable characters moves the highlight to the matching option (type-ahead)
Enter or Space selects the highlighted option and closes the list
Escape closes the list without changing the selection
A disabled trigger is removed from the tab order; disabled options are skipped by keyboard navigation
accessibility.targetSize: 44px project default (the trigger and every option are min-h-11)
notes: The trigger exposes role=combobox with aria-haspopup=listbox and aria-expanded; the popup (or its list) is role=listbox and each option is role=option.

The accessible name always includes the current value: aria-labelledby references the Field label (or a visually-hidden triggerLabel when standalone) plus the value text, so the trigger reads '<label>, <value>'.

Non-color redundancy: the selected option carries a check indicator and aria-selected in addition to any fill; the keyboard highlight adds font-medium as well as a muted background, so state survives forced-colors mode.

The trigger, popup, and options keep visible borders/indicators so forced-colors mode paints boundaries; the chevron is an inline SVG drawn with currentColor (not a background image) so it is not stripped there.
Option groups render role=group with an associated GroupLabel; option icons are decorative (aria-hidden) so the label carries the meaning.
